message:
[HIGH] security(patterns): close symlink-escape in discover_packs_from_dir (ft-fbee5)

`sandbox_resolve` was introduced in 33e84436 to reject `..` traversal and
symlink escape for `file:`-scheme pack loads, but `discover_packs_from_dir`
called `load_pack_from_file(path, None)`, skipping the sandbox entirely.

A hostile cloned repo could ship a symlink inside its own `.ft/patterns/`
pointing out-of-tree:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`entry.path().is_file()` follows the link (returns true), the loader opens
the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display())

...which can echo file excerpts back through serde's error messages. That
gives a hostile repo a read primitive over the victim's home directory at
session start.

Fix: pass `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root from `discover_packs_from_dir`
for both the flat-file and `<subdir>/rules.toml` discovery paths.
`sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes both ends and rejects any resolved
candidate outside `dir`.

Regression test: `discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape` places a symlink
inside a tempdir pointing at a file in a sibling tempdir, asserts
`discover_packs_from_dir` returns zero packs.

Filed as ft-fbee5. Residual risk (symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself
before `load_packs_from_config` joins it) tracked separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
